research.product: Microsoft Graph JSON batching over Outlook mail (mailFolders/messages)
research.interface: Microsoft Graph REST API v1.0, POST /$batch (https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/$batch)

official_sources.title: Combine multiple HTTP requests using JSON batching
official_sources.used_for: POST /v1.0/$batch endpoint; requests[] schema (id/method/url/headers/body) with unique ids (duplicate = 400), relative resource URLs, Content-Type required only when a body is present; responses[] schema (id/status/headers/body) explicitly unordered, correlated by id; batch-level 200 independent of per-subrequest status; 20-request batch size limit

official_sources.title: Microsoft Graph throttling guidance
official_sources.used_for: requests inside a batch are throttled individually: the batch returns 200 while throttled subrequests carry status 429 with a retry-after value in the subresponse; the documented recovery is to retry only the failed subrequests in a new batch after the longest retry-after; SDK auto-retry does not apply to batched requests

official_sources.title: List messages - Microsoft Graph v1.0
official_sources.used_for: GET /users/{id}/mailFolders/{id}/messages request path, Authorization Bearer header, OData query parameter support ($select/$top), 200 OK message collection with subject/receivedDateTime/from properties

official_sources.title: Paging Microsoft Graph data in your app
official_sources.used_for: @odata.nextLink must be used as returned until it stops appearing; the $skiptoken/$skip inside it is opaque and must not be extracted or modified; a page may contain zero results; original query parameters are already encoded into the nextLink

research.language_rationale: The exporter feeds a Node-based mail archiving service, and Graph's JSON batching is a plain REST envelope with no special SDK semantics; TypeScript on Node's built-in fetch/http stack (node:test harness, zero npm dependencies) is the idiomatic way to build and pin the raw $batch contract.

verified_facts: JSON batching is POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/$batch with a JSON object whose only required property is requests[].
Each subrequest carries a unique string id, an HTTP method, and a RELATIVE url (e.g. /users/{id}/mailFolders); duplicate ids fail the whole batch with 400.
Microsoft Graph supports at most 20 subrequests per batch.
The batch response property is responses[]; individual responses may appear in a different order than the requests and must be correlated by id; each carries its own status/headers/body.
A 200 on the batch itself does not mean subrequests succeeded; errors use the standard Graph error envelope per subresponse.

Subrequests are evaluated individually against throttling limits: a throttled subrequest fails with 429 and a retry-after value inside the batch while the batch returns 200; the guidance is to retry the failed subrequests in a new batch after the longest retry-after, and batched throttled requests are not retried automatically by the SDKs.
Messages listing is GET /users/{id}/mailFolders/{id}/messages returning a value[] of message resources (subject, from.emailAddress.{name,address}, receivedDateTime, isRead) and supports $select/$top.
Paged collections return @odata.nextLink whose token portion is opaque; clients must reuse the link as-is until it no longer appears, and a page may hold zero results.
